rubricContent.textProperty: When asked to decompose an amended clause, the response emits multiple distinct atomic obligations whenever the clause encodes distinct (subject, action, deadline, cadence) tuples. A clause that legitimately fans out into N obligations should not be flattened to one.

When asked about coverage of an obligation by the bank's existing policy, the response labels coverage as full / partial / missing / stale / contradicts and is willing to return 'missing' when no internal policy addresses the obligation, rather than inventing coverage.
Suggested edits to policy sections are concrete (current_text and suggested_text both present), preserve the section's tone and numbering, and cite the obligation(s) and source clause they address.

The judge surfaces non-obvious downstream effects (e.g., ICAAP recalibration, capital plan revision, Pillar 3 disclosure template, RMC standing agenda, training, board reporting cadence) — not only the direct policy edits.

Follow-up Q&A answers cite specific obligation ids (e.g. obl-…) and policy section ids (e.g. POL-…#sN). The agent refuses to invent citations and is willing to say the analysis does not address a question.
